meta.phase_title: FE external-contract consumption (final HTML audit phase)
meta.brief: docs/html-audit-phase-b-brief-2026-06-02-v1.1.md (ratified v1.1; provided inline by Vic this session — file is not present on disk in this repo; see meta.deliverable_gaps)
predecessor_phases: D (canonical per CLAUDE.md restatement; deliverable files not on disk in this repo — see meta.deliverable_gaps)
meta.scope: Static consumer enumeration against FE-authored code (public/, pages/, lib/, components/) and against the T4-served Funnelback stylesheet (provided inline), resolving the four consumer-enumeration questions handed forward from Phases E and D: E7 (typo IDs funnelbach-search-facets / funnelbach-search-body), E8 (tab-conditional CTA URL and label fixtures), E9 (listing-item--course modifier), dataListing + per-type *Data suffixes (D7).
meta.posture: Read-and-record. Documentary outcomes only, not cleanup-deciding. Under the three architectural anchors (visual-design-immutability; preserve-DOM-contract-by-default-pending-consumer-enumeration; stylesheet-as-binding-contract), consumer enumeration documents the contract; it does not license removal. Where a class or ID has no FE consumer, preservation by default is the rebuild posture; where the stylesheet binds a class, emit-and-align is the binding rebuild posture.
source_surfaces_in_scope: T4_SERVED_FUNNELBACK_STYLESHEET (v3.1.2 dated 2026-02-02; provided inline by Vic this session)
source_surfaces_out_of_scope: T4_PAGE_LAYOUT_PHP (constraint surface per Phase D D2)
FTL_TEMPLATES (emitting surface per Phase E, canonical)

anchors_in_effect.name: visual-design-immutability
anchors_in_effect.role: The rebuild makes no changes to the visual design. CSS class names referenced by stylesheets, markup envelopes whose styling depends on exact class assignment, and styled chrome are all preserve-verbatim.

anchors_in_effect.name: preserve-DOM-contract-by-default-pending-consumer-enumeration
anchors_in_effect.role: Every class name, data attribute, ARIA attribute, and selectable element with no audit-confirmed consumer is preserved by default. Removal requires consumer-enumeration-confirmed absence, not assumed absence.

anchors_in_effect.name: stylesheet-as-binding-contract
anchors_in_effect.role: The existing Funnelback stylesheet lives in T4, is dedicated to Funnelback styles, and is itself a binding restriction on the rebuild: everything the rebuild builds must align with the existing stylesheet. The rebuild does not rename styled classes; it emits what the stylesheet targets. Established 2026-06-02; queued for v19 formalization as a paired anchor with visual-design-immutability.

Operative scope, stop rules, finding taxonomy, and anchor framing for this audit were taken from the inline paste. The inline brief content is preserved in the session report. Recommendation: stage the brief into the repo retrospectively (either docs/ or audit/) so the canonical reference path resolves for future readers.

Phase B references D7 (dataListing / per-type *Data pattern hypothesis) via CLAUDE.md restatement; that dependency is satisfied. Other Phase D findings (D2 constraint framing, D3 visual-design-immutability anchor, D4 preserve-DOM-contract anchor, D8 cluster CONSUMER_MAP deferral) are likewise referenced via CLAUDE.md. brief_citation_corrections.e8_button_label_redhawk_hub_vs_explore_r: The Phase B brief (and the operator prompt) listed 'Redhawk Hub' among the four CTA button labels. Phase E E8 finding establishes that 'Redhawk Hub' is the *heading* of the Faculty & Staff CTA branch and 'Explore Resources' is the *button label* (per partial.ftl L138-158 and Phase E correction). Phase B grep covered both strings for defensiveness ('Redhawk Hub' and 'Explore Resources'); both returned 0 hits in FE source. The brief's labeling is internally consistent with the spirit of 'tab-conditional CTA content' but the per-string identification matches the heading layer not the button layer. Recorded for v19 amendments queue.

E7_typo_ids.what_is_emitted: id="funnelbach-search-facets" and id="funnelbach-search-body" (typo: 'funnelbach' instead of 'funnelback'). Class names on the same elements use correctly-spelled 'funnelback'.
E7_typo_ids.phase_b_question: Does any FE or T4 surface consume the typo'd IDs as DOM selectors at runtime?

phase_b_outcome.t4_shipped_js_dimension: OPEN RESIDUAL (RES1; recorded under residuals_open). Vic elected at synthesis time to close E7 with the T4-JS half as a named narrower residual rather than wait for live-page capture this session.

E8_cta_fixtures.phase_e_finding_reference: Phase E E8 + Phase E meta.brief_citation_corrections.cta_url_list — partial.ftl:130-172
E8_cta_fixtures.what_is_emitted: Four hardcoded tab-conditional CTA blocks. URLs (default → /visit, Programs → /academics/all-programs, Faculty & Staff → https://redhawks.sharepoint.com/sites/Intranet-Home, News → /newsroom). Headings (default → Visit our Campus, Programs → Discover Programs, Faculty & Staff → Redhawk Hub, News → Latest Updates). Button labels (default → Plan Your Visit, Programs → View Programs, Faculty & Staff → Explore Resources, News → See Latest News; exact labels per partial.ftl, not re-derived this session).
E8_cta_fixtures.phase_b_question: Does any FE code read, parse, or depend on the tab-conditional CTA content (URLs, headings, button labels)?

phase_b_outcome.implication_for_rebuild: Externalization of CTA content is purely an FTL/content concern; no FE-coordination dimension.

E9_listing_item_course_modifier.what_is_emitted: Self-id comment '<!-- results.courses::GenericView -->' at L47; news result-card article tag at L48 with classes 'listing-item listing-item--course listing-item--background-grey10 listing-item--color-black dataListing newsData'. The --course modifier is copy-paste residue from a courses template lineage.
E9_listing_item_course_modifier.phase_b_question: Does any FE JS hook or T4 stylesheet selector target .listing-item--course?

phase_b_outcome.fe_jsts_dimension: CONSUMER_ABSENT (finding B5). Seven .listing-item consumer sites exist in FE across five files; all target the base class or the .listing-item__title BEM element; none target the --course modifier.
phase_b_outcome.t4_stylesheet_dimension: CONSUMER_ABSENT (finding B6). Stylesheet defines styled modifiers for --people, --event, --person, --document, --social-media, --video, --promoted, --background-shadow; --course is conspicuously absent from the set.
phase_b_outcome.implication_for_rebuild: Residue with no consumer on any binding-evidence surface. Under preserve-DOM-contract anchor, emit by default; no stylesheet contract to align with for the --course modifier specifically.

dataListing_data_suffixes.phase_e_finding_reference: Phase E E20 (cross-renderer envelope contract) + Phase D D7 (camelCase hypothesis)
dataListing_data_suffixes.what_is_emitted: Cross-renderer shared .listing-item BEM envelope plus 'dataListing' class plus per-renderer per-type *Data suffix: fallback → genericData, news → newsData, law → lawData, programs → programData, people → peopleData.
dataListing_data_suffixes.phase_b_question: (Phase D D7 hypothesis) Does any FE JS hook or T4 stylesheet selector target .dataListing or any of the per-type *Data classes? D7 framed the camelCase pattern as suggestive of JS hook selectors more than visual or ARIA semantics; consumer enumeration is the binding evidence.

phase_b_outcome.fe_jsts_dimension: CONSUMER_ABSENT (finding B7). Zero hits for any of the six class names across FE source. No data-class attribute consumer pattern either.
phase_b_outcome.t4_stylesheet_dimension: CONSUMER_ABSENT (finding B8). No stylesheet rule targets any of the six class names.
phase_b_outcome.d7_hypothesis_status: Negatively confirmed on both binding-evidence surfaces.

method.rationale: Primary grep covers the four FE-authored directories per the brief's scope sharpening. Widened recheck is repo-wide case-insensitive across all source extensions (defensive — would catch a stray reference in tsx/jsx/json/html that the primary scope misses).

file_extension_inventory_in_scope_dirs.note: No .css/.scss/.html/.jsx files in public/, pages/, lib/, or components/. Repo-wide stylesheet search outside node_modules and .next returned zero — confirming the brief's repo-shape sharpening: any CSS-consumer question is routed to the T4-served Funnelback stylesheet by construction.

hits: package-lock.json: 7 matches against '@typescript-eslint/visitor-keys' substring — NOT the CTA URL
public/search-bundle.js:422 — '.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a' (known-dead file per CLAUDE.md)
public/js/modules/analytics-manager.js:51 — '.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a'
public/integration.js:990 — '.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a'
public/search-page-autocomplete.js:801 — '.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a'
components/ResultList.tsx:38 — '.fb-result h3 a, .search-result-item h3 a, .listing-item__title a'
listing-item_base_for_context.interpretation: All seven sites target the base class '.listing-item' or the BEM element '.listing-item__title'. None target the '--course' modifier.

data-class_attribute_pattern.interpretation: No 'data-class' attribute consumer pattern in FE source. Sanity check: other data-* attribute patterns DO exist (data-type, data-index on suggestion-item elements in public/search-page-autocomplete.js:389,408,468 and public/search-bundle.js:279,289,311) — confirming the grep does find data-* patterns where they exist; it does not find data-class because data-class is not in source.

stylesheet_enumeration_evidence.source: T4-served Funnelback Stylesheet (Version 3.1.2, dated 2025-01-05, Last Updated 2026-02-02 by Vic). Provided inline by Vic during this Phase B session.
stylesheet_enumeration_evidence.method: Manual full-read of the stylesheet content (the stylesheet is hand-grokable at single-pass-read scale). Token searches recorded below.

dot_funnelback-search__body_CLASS_select.note: All three selector occurrences are within .stencils__main scopes; rule bodies are commented out. The selector is matched (class-on-element binding exists) but no active style rules apply.

dot_funnelback-search__side_CLASS_select.note: Same pattern as __body — selector matched, rules commented out.

dot_funnelback-search_CLASS_selector.note: Most rule bodies commented out within .stencils__main scopes; some active sibling-selector rules exist elsewhere.

dot_funnelback-search-facets-breadcrumb_.note: Active styled selectors. This is a DIFFERENT element (the breadcrumb element above the facets sidebar, not the typo'd ID's element).

dot_funnelback-facet_CLASS_selector.note: Active styled selectors. This is the facet block element, not the typo'd ID's element.

listing-item--course.note: No stylesheet rule targets the modifier.

absent_from_set: .listing-item--background-grey10 (sibling modifier on the same news article element)
.listing-item--color-black (sibling modifier on the same news article element)

residuals_open.scope: E7 T4-shipped JS / live-page DOM consumer dimension
residuals_open.description: The T4-shipped JS dimension of E7 (whether Squiz framework JS or T4 page-layout chrome JS targets #funnelbach-search-facets or #funnelbach-search-body at runtime, or whether any inline <script> block in the T4-rendered HTML references the typo'd IDs) was not closed in this session. The stylesheet half of the T4-side check is closed in B2 (CONSUMER_ABSENT). The remaining half requires a live-page DOM/inline-script check.
needed_capture: Browser console: document.querySelectorAll('[id*="funnelbach"], [id*="funnelback-search-facets"], [id*="funnelback-search-body"]') — record count and matches
View-source Ctrl-F for 'funnelbach' across all inline <script> blocks in the T4-rendered HTML
If feasible, fetch and grep any T4-served JS bundle URLs (visible via Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('script[src]')).map(s => s.src)) for 'funnelbach'
residuals_open.rebuild_impact: If T4-JS consumer exists: rebuild coordinates the spelling correction with T4 chrome modifications. If absent: rebuild corrects freely. Either outcome leaves Phase B's documentary classification intact; only the rebuild's coordination plan changes. No Phase B finding category changes regardless of outcome.

residuals_closed_in_session.scope: Stylesheet-alignment cross-check (the brief's named one carried-forward documentary residual)
residuals_closed_in_session.description: Brief v1.1's 'Stylesheet-alignment cross-check' section carved the visual-dimension cross-check for E9 (.listing-item--course) and dataListing/per-type *Data classes — i.e., which classes are live-styled vs. residue the stylesheet ignores — as one named carried-forward dependency past Phase B execution, pending stylesheet source landing. Vic provided the T4-served Funnelback stylesheet inline during this session; the cross-check fires in-session and resolves CONSUMER_ABSENT across all three subject class sets (B6 and B8). The brief's projected closure framing of 'closed-with-one-documentary-residual' is updated by execution to 'closed-with-one-narrower-documentary-residual' — RES1 (E7 T4-JS) is the only remaining residual, a smaller surface than the stylesheet-alignment cross-check the brief carried.

findings.title: E7 typo IDs have no FE consumer (public/, pages/, lib/, components/)
findings.evidence: grep -rn 'funnelbach-search-facets' public/ pages/ lib/ components/ returns 0 hits. grep -rn 'funnelbach-search-body' public/ pages/ lib/ components/ returns 0 hits. Repo-wide case-insensitive recheck across .js, .ts, .tsx, .jsx, .json, .html, .css, .scss (excluding node_modules, .next, audit, docs) for the bare substring 'funnelbach' returns 0 hits. The correctly-spelled equivalents ('funnelback-search-facets', 'funnelback-search-body') also return 0 hits across all FE source — confirming FE does not bind either spelling of these IDs as JS-side or CSS-side selectors.
findings.implication: Under preserve-DOM-contract anchor, no FE consumer means the rebuild's FE side is free of any binding to either spelling. The conditional T4-side check from the brief fires; it resolves partially via B2 (stylesheet) and remains open as RES1 (T4-shipped JS).

findings.title: E7 typo IDs are not bound by the T4-served Funnelback stylesheet (either spelling)
findings.evidence: Stylesheet grep against the v3.1.2 source provided inline: 'funnelbach' substring 0 occurrences; '#funnelbach-search-facets' / '#funnelbach-search-body' ID selectors 0 occurrences; '#funnelback-search-facets' / '#funnelback-search-body' ID selectors (correctly spelled) 0 occurrences. The stylesheet does target CLASSES on the same elements — '.funnelback-search__body' (3 selector occurrences, rule bodies commented out within .stencils__main scopes) and '.funnelback-search__side' (2 occurrences, same pattern) — but does not target IDs in either spelling.
findings.implication: The T4-stylesheet half of E7's T4-side check closes as CONSUMER_ABSENT. Under stylesheet-as-binding-contract, no rule to align with on the ID surface. The class-on-same-element contract (.funnelback-search__body, .funnelback-search__side) is still binding per the anchor regardless of ID-side outcome. The remaining T4-side surface (T4-shipped JS / page-layout chrome JS) is recorded as RES1.

findings.title: E7 T4-shipped JS dimension recorded as named open residual (RES1)
findings.evidence: Per Phase B brief v1.1's 'Scope (conditional)' section, the T4-side runtime DOM check fires when the FE-side grep returns negative. The T4-side check has two sub-surfaces: T4-served stylesheet (resolved B2, CONSUMER_ABSENT) and T4-shipped JS (open). Vic elected at synthesis time to close E7 with the T4-JS half as a named narrower residual rather than wait for live-page capture this session.
findings.implication: Closes E7 partially. The rebuild's coordination plan with T4 chrome cannot be finalized until the T4-JS half resolves, but this does not block any other Phase B finding or the HTML audit sequence closure. The residual is recorded as RES1 with explicit capture instructions.

findings.title: E8 CTA URLs and labels have no FE consumer (read, parse, or depend)
findings.evidence: grep -rn for each of the four URLs ('/visit', '/academics/all-programs', 'redhawks.sharepoint.com', '/newsroom') and each of the five-plus heading and button label strings ('Visit our Campus', 'Discover Programs', 'Redhawk Hub', 'Explore Resources', 'Latest Updates') across public/, pages/, lib/, components/ returned 0 hits per token. The only '/visit' hits in the repo (7 total) are '@typescript-eslint/visitor-keys' substring matches in package-lock.json — not the CTA URL. (Note: the brief listed 'Redhawk Hub' as a button label; per Phase E E8 'Redhawk Hub' is the heading and 'Explore Resources' is the button label of the Faculty & Staff section. Both strings were grepped for defensiveness; both absent.)
findings.implication: Externalization of CTA content is purely an FTL/content concern; there is no FE-coordination dimension. The rebuild can move CTA content to profile config, JSON fixture, or any other emit-side location without FE coordination. Under preserve-DOM-contract, the rendered anchor elements remain user-clickable navigation; no FE-side dependency to preserve. CTA content is not a styled-class question per the brief, so the stylesheet constraint does not bear; CONSUMER_ABSENT closes E8 on its only relevant dimension.

findings.title: E9 modifier .listing-item--course has no FE JS-hook consumer; base .listing-item consumers all target base or __title BEM element
findings.evidence: grep -rn 'listing-item--course' public/ pages/ lib/ components/ returns 0 hits. Repo-wide case-insensitive recheck across all source extensions (excluding node_modules, .next, audit, docs) returns 0 hits. For context, grep for the base 'listing-item' class returned 7 consumer sites across 5 files: public/search-bundle.js:422 (known-dead file per CLAUDE.md), public/js/modules/analytics-manager.js:51 + :133 + :138, public/integration.js:990, public/search-page-autocomplete.js:801, components/ResultList.tsx:38. All seven sites target either the base '.listing-item' class or the BEM element '.listing-item__title' selector pattern; none target the '--course' modifier.
findings.implication: The FE JS-hook dimension of E9 closes as CONSUMER_ABSENT. The five active sites + two more (analytics-manager :133, :138) all target the base BEM block or the __title element — confirming the base envelope is the binding FE-internal contract and the --course modifier carries no FE consumer. Combined with B6 (stylesheet dimension), E9 closes fully.

findings.title: E9 modifier .listing-item--course has no T4-served stylesheet consumer; styled-modifier set on .listing-item is enumerated
findings.evidence: Stylesheet grep for '.listing-item--course' returns 0 occurrences. The stylesheet defines styled modifiers on the .listing-item BEM block: --people, --event, --person, --document, --social-media, --video, --promoted, --background-shadow (all heavily styled with active rule bodies). The --course modifier is conspicuously absent from this set. The two sibling modifiers on the same news article element per Phase E E9 evidence (--background-grey10, --color-black) are likewise absent from the stylesheet — they are residue at the cross-renderer envelope level, not just at --course.
findings.implication: Under stylesheet-as-binding-contract, no rule to align with on .listing-item--course. Under preserve-DOM-contract, the modifier is preserved by default. Combined with B5, .listing-item--course is residue with no behavioral or visual contract on any binding-evidence surface. The rebuild's posture is emit-by-default; a later optimization pass could strip --course without behavioral or visual consequence (and likewise the two sibling --background-grey10 / --color-black modifiers on the same element, though those are outside Phase B's direct scope).

findings.title: dataListing and per-type *Data suffixes have no FE JS-hook consumer (D7 hypothesis negatively confirmed for FE)
findings.evidence: grep -rn for each of 'dataListing', 'genericData', 'lawData', 'newsData', 'programData', 'peopleData' across public/, pages/, lib/, components/ returns 0 hits per token. Repo-wide case-insensitive recheck returns 0 hits per token. The 'data-class' attribute-consumer pattern (would target the class via attribute selector) returns 0 hits. Sanity check: other data-* attribute consumer patterns DO exist in FE source (data-type and data-index on suggestion-item elements in public/search-page-autocomplete.js:389, :408, :468 and public/search-bundle.js:279, :289, :311) — confirming the grep methodology surfaces data-* patterns where they exist; the camelCase D7 classes simply are not consumed.
findings.implication: Phase D D7's hypothesis that the camelCase paired generic-plus-per-type pattern 'suggests JS hook selectors more than visual or ARIA semantics' is *negatively confirmed* for the FE source surface. No FE JS hook keys off these classes. Combined with B8 (stylesheet dimension), the D7 hypothesis is fully closed.

findings.title: dataListing and per-type *Data suffixes have no T4-served stylesheet consumer (D7 hypothesis fully closed)
findings.evidence: Stylesheet grep for 'dataListing', 'genericData', 'lawData', 'newsData', 'programData', 'peopleData' returns 0 occurrences per token. None of the six class names are bound by any stylesheet rule.
findings.implication: Phase D D7 closes on both binding-evidence surfaces: neither JS-hook selectors (B7) nor stylesheet selectors (B8) consume these classes. Under stylesheet-as-binding-contract, no rule to align with — there is no emit-and-align obligation specifically for these classes. Under preserve-DOM-contract, the classes are preserved by default. The 'camelCase pattern suggests JS hook selectors' hypothesis from D7 has no empirical support on either binding surface. The rebuild's posture is emit-by-default; the classes carry no behavioral or visual contract.

findings.title: Stylesheet-alignment cross-check fired in-session — the brief's projected residual collapses to a narrower one
findings.evidence: Brief v1.1's 'Stylesheet-alignment cross-check (carried-forward documentary residual)' section explicitly carved the visual-dimension cross-check for .listing-item--course and the dataListing / per-type *Data classes as the one named residual carried past Phase B execution, pending stylesheet source landing. Vic provided the T4-served Funnelback stylesheet inline during this Phase B session; the cross-check fires in-session and resolves CONSUMER_ABSENT for all three subject class sets (outputs: B6 and B8).
findings.implication: The HTML audit sequence's projected closure framing of 'closed-with-one-documentary-residual' (the stylesheet-alignment cross-check) is superseded by in-session execution. The remaining residual after Phase B execution is RES1 (E7 T4-shipped JS) — a smaller surface than the stylesheet-alignment cross-check the brief carried, since RES1 only covers a partial dimension of E7 rather than the full visual-dimension cross-check for three subject class sets. Closure framing for the v19 amendments queue: 'HTML audit sequence closes with one narrower residual.'

findings.title: HTML audit sequence (A then C then E then D then B) closes with one named residual (RES1)
findings.evidence: Phase A (canonical: v2 markdown + v3 JSON), Phase C (canonical), Phase E (canonical), Phase D (canonical per CLAUDE.md restatement; deliverables not on disk per GAP2), and Phase B (this audit). All emitting and consuming surfaces enumerated to brief-scope. Prior phase closures: Phase A F2 closed via Phase E E5; Phase A F10/F11 closed via Phase C C6/C7; Phase C C8 closed via Phase E E11. Phase B closures: E7 partial via B1/B2 (FE absent + stylesheet absent), with B3 noting RES1; E8 full via B4; E9 full via B5/B6; D7 full via B7/B8.
findings.implication: Phase B is the final HTML audit phase per the brief; the sequence closes for documentation purposes. The v19 project-instructions revision is the queued amendment that formalizes closure, the stylesheet-as-binding-contract paired anchor, and RES1. The CLAUDE.md update is the queued single-pass edit list. Both sub-phase 5 outputs happen outside this session per the operator prompt.

phase_e_E7.via: B1 (FE absent) + B2 (T4 stylesheet absent) + B3 (T4-shipped JS open as RES1)
phase_e_E7.note: Two of three E7 surfaces resolved CONSUMER_ABSENT; the third (T4-shipped JS) is RES1. Rebuild can correct typo on the FE side and stylesheet side without coordination; coordination requirement with T4 chrome is contingent on RES1 outcome.

phase_e_E8.note: CTA fixtures have no FE consumer; externalization is purely an FTL/content concern. The brief's minor labeling discrepancy ('Redhawk Hub' vs. 'Explore Resources') was recorded but does not affect closure since both strings were grepped and both absent.

phase_e_E9.note: .listing-item--course is residue with no consumer on any binding-evidence surface. Preserved by default; later optimization could strip without consequence.

phase_d_D7.note: Phase D D7's working hypothesis that the camelCase paired generic-plus-per-type pattern suggests JS hook selectors is negatively confirmed on both binding-evidence surfaces. dataListing and the per-type *Data classes have no behavioral or visual contract. Preserved by default.

stylesheet_alignment_cross_check.via: B9 (records the in-session resolution) + B6 + B8 (the actual closures)
stylesheet_alignment_cross_check.note: Brief v1.1's named one carried-forward documentary residual collapses to RES1 (E7 T4-JS), a narrower surface.

claude_md_single_pass.description: The five edits CLAUDE.md needs after Phase B closure (per brief v1.1's 'Amendment queue' section). Happens in sub-phase 5, outside this session.
edits_anticipated: Current-audit-phase line: 'Phase B (FE external-contract consumption) is the next-and-final HTML audit phase' becomes 'HTML audit sequence (A then C then E then D then B) is closed with one named residual (RES1: E7 T4-shipped JS). Phase B completed 2026-06-02.'
Add stylesheet-as-binding-contract to Key architectural facts as a paired anchor with visual-design-immutability
Update dataListing / per-type *Data D7 framing to record the Phase B closure (D7 hypothesis negatively confirmed on both surfaces)
Companion-docs v18 then v19 reference update

Audit-documents list gains the Phase B pair, session report, and ratified brief (and re-records the Phase D missing-from-disk gap as a known item if Vic decides to leave the Phase D deliverables in the broader project context rather than stage them)

v18_to_v19_project_instructions_revision.description: Folds in Phase B findings, the HTML-audit-sequence closure, and the stylesheet-as-binding-contract constraint as a new key architectural anchor paired with visual-design-immutability. Happens outside this session.
elements_to_fold: HTML-audit-sequence closure (A then C then E then D then B) with named residual RES1
GAP1 (missing brief on disk) and GAP2 (missing Phase D deliverables on disk) as housekeeping items
Brief citation correction for E8 button label ('Redhawk Hub' is heading, 'Explore Resources' is button label)

sign_off.execution_shape: Single-phase per brief v1.1's ratified execution shape. No mid-write splitting. The escape hatch (broader-than-four-handoff-items surface) did not fire — grep returned exactly the four items asked for. The cross-check residual was folded into in-session resolution after Vic provided the stylesheet inline. The T4-JS dimension of E7 was elected as the one open residual.
sign_off.posture_adherence: No FE application code, dependency, or build configuration modified. No FTL modification. No stylesheet modification. Only deliverable writes under audit/** scoped permissions. No runtime captures taken in-session (stylesheet was provided inline by Vic; live-page DOM capture for RES1 was deferred per Vic's election).
